title: Python version requirements listed in pyproject.toml may not be correct
body:
The `pyproject.toml` file lists the `required-python` version as greater or equal to 3.9. The `dependencies` list also lists that we depend on `python_version<'3.11'`. This means that we depend on a version of Python greater or equal to 3.9 and less than 3.11. However, PR #401, which was merged into the main branch, added Python 3.12 support. PR #401 also closed issue #397 which was a request to add support for Python version 3.12 and greater.

The line in `pyproject.toml` that specifies we need a Python version less than 3.11 comes from this string: `"tomli>=1.2.3; python_version<'3.11'",`. Note that it is bundled with the dependency on [tomli](https://pypi.org/project/tomli/), which suggests that they are related. Newer versions of `tomli` do support Python versions greater or equal to 3.11 though, so perhaps we need to increase the minimum version of `tomli` ([Here](https://github.com/hukkin/tomli/blob/master/pyproject.toml) is a link to the `tomli` `pyproject.toml` file).

Is it incorrect that the `pyproject.toml` file says we require a Python version less than 3.11? Would any other steps need to be taken to support newer versions of Python?

I'd be happy to work on a PR for this once I better understand the situation. Thanks!

comments.body: @NicholasBHubbard thanks, it's a bug and we support python >= 3.9 and on. feel free to open a PR, and it would be a nice fix.
Closing, see pr for reason. Apologies for the noise
